title: Taryn Clancy
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Clancy}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:Taryn_Clancy_AJ7.jpg]] |name=Taryn Clancy |homeworld= |birth=[[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]][[Star Wars Adventure Journal 7|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 7]] ("Captain Taryn Clancy" sidebar) |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=Black |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Kal Clancy]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We made a deal. The New Republic can use my ship, but no one's flying it but me.|Taryn Clancy|{{JournalCite|7|Retreat from Coruscant}}}} '''Taryn Clancy''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Courier/Legends|courier]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] operating during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Born in [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]] to [[Kal Clancy]], captain of the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Lassen]]'', Taryn often tried to join his crew, once even resorting to stowing away, and he finally let her join as his copilot when she was sixteen. However, this led to a deterioration of their relationship. Later, she joined the [[Core Courier Service]]. On her ship, the [[Messenger (starship)|''Messenger'']], Clancy regularly flew a delivery route between [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coriallis]]. On one such mission shortly after [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], she and her first mate, [[Del Sato]], were stranded on Coruscant during its [[Second Battle of Coruscant|bombardment]] by a newly resurgent [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. As captain of one of several grounded civilian vessels, Clancy was brought to New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], who had been tasked with coordinating the government's evacuation of Coruscant. Under Bel Iblis' orders, Clancy helped [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jak Bremen]], Director of Security for the New Republic [[Inner Council (New Republic)|Inner Council]], to sneak past the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockading]] the planet and bring important information to the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic fleet]]. During the eventful escape, Clancy not only had to contend with Imperial checkpoints and a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] planted in her cargo hold, but the surly personality of Bremen himself. ==Biography== ===Early life=== Taryn Clancy was the daughter of [[Kal Clancy]], a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. From a young age, she dreamed of commanding her own [[Starship/Legends|ship]], and constantly begged her father to let her accompany him on cargo runs. On one occasion, she stowed away in the cargo hold of Kal's ship, the ''[[Lassen]]'', only to be discovered by a [[Load lifter (machine)|load lifter]] and returned home in shame. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Kal allowed Taryn, then 16, to accompany him on his trips. Despite the challenges inherent in serving aboard her father's ship, Taryn's determination eventually won her the formal position of copilot. All the while, however, her relationship with her father worsened. When she failed to live up to his expectations, he was disappointed, and made little effort to conceal his feelings. This had a profound effect on his daughter, and for years afterward she couldn't avoid judging herself the same way he had judged her. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Clancy endured an especially harsh critique by her demanding father; stung, she entered a [[Core Courier Service]] office she'd come across and in short order passed two exams and a flight test. She was hired then and there, but her successful career thereafter still seemed insufficient to earn her father's esteem. Unbeknownst to Taryn, Kal was proud of her, although he didn't consider mail delivery challenging enough to truly help her grow into the full potential he believed she had. Eventually, her superiors at Core Courier Service promoted her to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]], giving her a ship—the [[720 light freighter/Legends|Ghtroc 720]] [[Messenger (starship)|''Messenger'']]. [[Del Sato]], a [[Courier/Legends|courier]] with thirty years' experience, signed on as her copilot, satisfied with taking her orders despite never having attained a command of his own. ===The flight from Coruscant=== {{Quote|What's up?''"
"''Us, I hope.|Del Sato and Taryn Clancy|}} In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], during her fourth run as the ''Messenger''{{'s}} captain, Clancy traveled to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to deliver [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]]. As she was concluding the dropoff, alarm klaxons sounded, indicating an attack on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was underway. Immediately recognizing the danger of being stranded beneath the [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]]—or worse, grounded in the midst of a battle—Clancy rushed back to the ''Messenger'', where Sato had already prepped the vessel for takeoff. Her request to depart was denied, however, and soon after, she noticed distant [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire high in the sky, indicating a battle was underway. [[File:AJ7-p171.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Commander Voldt parts ways with Clancy.]] That night, Clancy impatiently strode into the palace to demand answers from someone official. Once inside, she encountered [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Jak Bremen]], who unexpectedly led her to [[General/Legends|General]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]], a man Clancy knew by reputation but whose physical appearance was only vaguely familiar to her. Bel Iblis explained that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] had gained the upper hand in [[Second Battle of Coruscant|the battle]], and that the New Republic was pulling out to minimize civilian casualties. He went on to commandeer her vessel for the purpose of sneaking a datacard past the Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockading]] the planet; this datacard carried intelligence on the battle and coordinates for the New Republic government's rendezvous. Clancy reacted forcefully when Bremen, who had been assigned to accompany them, remarked that he should pilot the vessel during the escape. She insisted on flying her own ship, and managed to get past the fleet in orbit over Coruscant and safely into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]—though [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] knocked out their [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] just before the jump. En route to [[Coriallis]], the ''Messenger'' was [[Interdiction field/Legends|pulled from hyperspace]] by the Imperial [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] ''[[Requital]]''. [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Voldt]] led the Imperial inspection team, questioning Clancy on her destination. Clancy informed Voldt that they were headed for Coriallis and that they had come from Coruscant. However, she lied that the ''Messenger'' had never landed on the galactic capital, claiming to have arrived in the system in the middle of the Imperial assault and promptly leaving to avoid being caught up in a combat zone. Voldt insisted on inspecting the cargo hold, and almost caught Clancy in the lie when he inquired as to where the mail bound for Coruscant was. Clancy struggled for an explanation; the Coruscant mail had obviously been offloaded. Sato interrupted and eased Voldt's suspicions, saying that he moved the Coruscant mail to one side and had brought the crates bound for Coriallis to the front for ease of delivery. Sato and Bremen had altered a batch of mail already aboard to have Coruscant addresses on them to provide a credible cover story. Voldt searched the Coruscant mail crates, then ordered his troops to search the rest of the boxes to confirm that they contained mail. After checking the ''Messenger''{{'s}} permits, Voldt permitted them to leave. Over the course of the following week, they reached Coriallis and proceeded to make several short hyperspace jumps programmed by Bremen—most of which took them to destinations Clancy couldn't identify. During the journey, Bremen demonstrated a pronounced lack of confidence in Clancy and Sato, believing Bel Iblis ought to have removed the ''Messenger''{{'s}} crew entirely; in response, Clancy—and especially Sato—came to resent Bremen's haughty and secretive attitude. ===Finding the fleet=== {{Quote|Captain Clancy, it's no secret I don't think you or Del Sato should have been allowed on this mission. You're civilians, and more of a hindrance than a help. You can't be expected to make the kind of split-second decisions needed to keep us out of trouble.|Jak Bremen to Taryn Clancy|}} [[File:AJ7-p165.jpg|thumb|right|230px|The ''Messenger'' under attack]] Once the ship had reached the supposed rendezvous—in an area Clancy assumed to be the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderlands]] separating Imperial-controlled and New Republic-controlled [[Realspace/Legends|space]]—it drifted in space for hours waiting for the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic fleet]]. Instead, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and New Republic ally [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] arrived in a [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray blastboat]] with new coordinates for Bremen, who appeared suspicious of her motives and timing. Moments later, an Imperial [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class cruiser]] dropped out of hyperspace nearby. Clancy prepared to use the new coordinates delivered by Jade to escape, but Bremen objected, claiming Jade could have set a trap for them. When Jade retaliated against the TIE fighters launched by the cruiser, Clancy decided Bremen was mistaken and made the hyperspace jump. Roughly an hour later, the ''Messenger'' reverted from hyperspace near the New Republic fleet, and was hailed by [[Arboga]], the captain of the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Hope (MC80 Star Cruiser)|''Hope'']]. As the ''Messenger'' approached the cruiser to drop off Bremen and the datacard, Clancy was approached by Bremen, who had found a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] planted in one of the mail crates in the hold by Voldt. The ''Carrack''-class cruiser appeared moments later, following the beacon's signal, and opened fire on the ''Messenger'' while it was still far away from the fleet and vulnerable. As the ship's shields began to fail, Clancy surprised Bremen by engaging a backup [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]] she had brought online after they'd cleared Coruscant. The backup generator bought them enough time to get within the New Republic fleet's turbolaser range, where the Imperial cruiser was quickly destroyed by both capital ship fire and [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] from [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] fighters. Colonel Bremen, impressed at their performance during the ordeal, invited Clancy and Sato to join the New Republic. Sato declined, citing his impending retirement, but Clancy replied that she had to complete her deliveries, and that she'd consider his offer. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|We're always looking for good pilots, and if you've got a mind for a career change, the New Republic could use someone like you.|Jak Bremen to Taryn Clancy|}} Clancy earned recognition and praise from her superiors at Core Courier Service for her abilities and commitment. A skilled pilot and [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogator]], Clancy could also repair her own ship at a professional level of expertise. She knew tricks in shield modification that enabled her to give the ''Messenger'' a backup shield system using a bare minimum of parts, a feat that impressed even the stolid Bremen. Clancy's father had a profound—and not entirely positive—influence on her. While he taught her most of what she knew, including the important tricks involved in cobbling together her backup shield generator, he frequently made patronizing remarks or sighed in response to perceived failures on her part. Despite her best efforts to shut out such feelings, she was haunted throughout her life by a persistent fear of what he would think of her response to various circumstances. In particular, she worried that she stayed with the safe, comfortable mail delivery job due to a lack of courage. Following her adventure on behalf of the New Republic, however, she seemed newly unconcerned with what Kal might think. ==Behind the scenes== Taryn Clancy is the protagonist of the short story "[[Retreat from Coruscant]]," which was originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 7|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 7]] and later reprinted in ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'' and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]]. The story is told from her perspective in the third person. Clancy was created by [[Laurie Burns]] and illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]]. "Retreat from Coruscant" is the only source in which the character appears. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|7|Retreat from Coruscant|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Clancy, Taryn}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Spacers]]
